seed_problem: Place watchtowers on a forest graph to cover every cell within radius r, minimizing total cost; score = baseline_cost / achieved_cost.
mutation: input+output: generalize the geometric radius-cover to a weighted connected graph with per-vertex installation costs (weighted minimum-cost dominating-set-within-radius = weighted set cover), skinned as placing chair-lift base stations on a ski-resort zone graph so every zone is within comfort ski-time R of a station, minimizing total installation cost.
title: Alpine Lift Network Coverage
strategies: cost-effective greedy set cover: repeatedly add the station maximizing newly-served-zones per cost
greedy + redundancy pruning: drop stations whose served zones are already covered by others
randomized multi-start greedy with different cost-effectiveness tie-breaks, keep the cheapest valid plan
LP-relaxation / rounding of the covering LP
